discover-for-you-empty: Subscribe to a few podcasts first — we use their categories to recommend similar ones.
discover-charts-country-hint: Enter a country code (e.g. de, us, gb) to see the top chart.
discover-locale-hint: Your country/language is used to filter recommendations.
auto-transcode-opus-explanation: Automatically convert downloaded episodes to Opus format. This can reduce storage usage by up to 50% compared to MP3. Requires ffmpeg to be installed on the server.
use-one-cover-for-all-episodes: Use one cover for all episodes
use-one-cover-for-all-episodes-explanati: When enabled, do not download a separate cover image for each episode. The shared podcast directory cover (image.jpg) will be used instead, saving disk space.
rescan-audio-files-description: PodFetch will rescan all audio files to update metadata like chapters. This can take a while depending on the number of podcast episodes.
title: Title
following-file-uploaded: The following file will be uploaded:
delete-podcast-with-files-body: Are you sure you want to delete the podcast {{name}} and all downloaded files?
delete-podcast-without-files-body: Are you sure you want to delete the podcast {{name}}? No files will be deleted.
download-all-missing-explanation: Queues every episode of this podcast that has not been downloaded yet.
redownload-missing-files-explanation: For episodes the database shows as downloaded but whose file is gone from disk, download the file again.
refresh-local-state-explanation: Check the filesystem and clear the "downloaded" flag for episodes whose file is missing. No downloads happen.
delete-all-downloads-explanation: Remove every downloaded file for this podcast and reset the episodes to "not downloaded". Episode metadata is kept so you can download them again.
confirm-refresh-local-state-body: This will clear the "downloaded" flag for every episode of this podcast whose file is missing on disk. No files are changed.
confirm-delete-all-downloads-body: This removes every downloaded file for this podcast. Episode metadata is kept, so they can be re-downloaded later.
are-you-sure-you-want-to-delete-this-use: Are you sure you want to delete  the user {{name}}?
password-too-weak: Password too weak. A secure password contains at least 8 characters, a number, a lowercase and an uppercase letter.
not-admin-or-uploader: You are not an admin or an uploader
no-tags-created: No tags created yet. Use the form above to add one.
number-of-podcasts-to-download: Number of initial podcasts to download
replace-invalid-characters-description: Replace illegal characters in filenames with a dash
days-to-keep-explanation: Reflects the number of days a podcast is kept, i.e., stored on the server. This setting is only active if auto-purge is enabled or Run purge now is clicked.
auto-update-explanation: PodFetch will automatically check for new episodes when the page is visited. If this option is disabled, podcasts will need to be manually updated to check for new episodes. The update interval can be configured via Environment Variable.
number-of-podcasts-to-download-explanati: Returns the number of podcast episodes that are downloaded when a podcast is initially added. If 0, only the database entries are created. An option like 10000 archives all podcast episodes.
auto-download-explanation: If this option is activated, the episodes will be downloaded automatically when a podcast is added
default-episode-format-explanation: Specifies the default format for the episodes. This only affects the folder structure and its naming.
standard-podcast-format-explanation: Specifies the standard format for the podcasts. This only affects the folder structure and naming.
standard-episode-format-explanation: Specifies the standard format for the episodes. This only affects the folder structure and naming.
colon-replacement-explanation: Specifies by which character the colon should be replaced. This is useful because the colon cannot be used in the file name.
playlist-page-description: Create mixed queues from episodes across multiple podcasts.
playlist-search-help: Search episodes above and tap one to add it to the playlist.
you-already-listened: You already listened to the episode <name/>. Do you want to restart playing?
stats-no-data: No listening data in this range.
UPDATE_OF_ADMIN_NOT_ALLOWED: Changing the admin account {{username}} is not allowed
media-controls-hint: Use the controls below for play/pause, seek and speed.
playlist-name-description: Give your playlist a name so you can quickly find it later.
cast-section-description: Chromecast devices visible to PodFetch. Personal devices belong to you and only show up while your agent is running. Shared devices are registered by an administrator for the household.
cast-no-devices-empty-state: No Chromecasts visible. Either run an agent on your home LAN, or wait for an admin to register a household device.
cast-discover-informational: Informational only — discovered devices are not saved unless an agent reports them.
cast-discover-empty: No Chromecasts found on this network.
cast-how-to-run-agent-description: Run the PodFetch agent on a machine on your home LAN to discover and control Chromecasts. The agent connects back to this server using your API key.
cast-how-to-run-agent-api-key-here: Use the API key shown above.
cast-control-not-available: Cast control is not available yet for this session.
cast-no-stream-url: This episode has no stream URL.
cast-ended-error: Cast session ended due to an error.
